data.title: As a sprint orchestrator, I receive a structured escalation signal when implementation-plan cannot satisfy success criteria
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Implementation-plan gains a REPLAN_ESCALATE output signal that fires when it cannot produce a diff plan satisfying the parent epic/story success criteria with high confidence.
**Why**: Today, when implementation-plan encounters ambiguous or contradicted success criteria, it either guesses or fails silently. The calling orchestrator has no structured way to know that the plan gap is at the intent level (requiring brainstorm) rather than the task level.
**Scope**:
- IN: REPLAN_ESCALATE: brainstorm signal format, emission logic in implementation-plan, explanation payload describing the ambiguity
- OUT: Sprint routing logic for the signal (separate epic 9d3e-957d), brainstorm invocation itself, user confirmation UX

## Done Definitions
- When this story is complete, implementation-plan emits a REPLAN_ESCALATE: brainstorm signal with a human-readable explanation when it determines that success criteria cannot be satisfied given current codebase state
  ← Satisfies: "it emits REPLAN_ESCALATE: brainstorm with an explanation of the ambiguity"
- When this story is complete, the calling orchestrator can parse the REPLAN_ESCALATE signal from implementation-plan output to determine that brainstorm escalation is needed
  ← Satisfies: "The calling orchestrator is responsible for routing this signal"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations
- [Maintainability] Signal format must be documented in the output contract (plugins/dso/docs/contracts/)
- [Testing] Needs behavioral test: invoke implementation-plan with contradicted SC, assert REPLAN_ESCALATE emitted

## Escalation Policy
**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. "High confidence" means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.
